purpose: Legacy node naming reference for the historical fixed cluster identity. New provider-backed clusters must use a separate reusable provider-backed naming convention.

appliesTo: legacy compatibility checks that explicitly target sc-* nodes
doesNotApplyTo: C1 and later provider-backed infrastructure profiles

namingRules.toolNameComposition: proxmox-k3s builds final node names as <cluster_name>-<configured_node_name> unless the configured name already starts with the same prefix.

methodologicalRationale: The benchmark suite already contains validated topology compositions and historical evidence based on sc-master-01, sc-app-01, and sc-app-02.
Keeping the same deterministic naming convention for provisioned clusters avoids unnecessary refactoring before validating proxmox-k3s.
The infrastructure profile remains mandatory because identical node names do not imply identical CPU, memory, storage, provisioning mode, or installed add-ons.
operationalRequirements: Do not create new provider-backed clusters with the sc-* prefix.
Do not delete or recreate the historical fixed-cluster nodes through provider-backed lifecycle commands.
Use this convention only when interpreting historical C0/B0 evidence or explicitly targeting the historical fixed cluster.

riskControls.vmNameCollisionRisk: Before provisioning, ensure that Proxmox does not already contain active VMs with the same generated names for the target experiment, or intentionally delete/reuse the target cluster according to the lifecycle policy.
riskControls.nodeIdentityRisk: Do not infer hardware equivalence from node names; always use the declared infrastructureProfileId in run metadata and reports.
riskControls.futurePortabilityRisk: If arbitrary external clusters must be supported later, introduce label-based placement or node-role abstraction in a new cycle without rewriting C0.
